style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#BBDEFB;strokeColor=#1565C0;strokeWidth=2;arcSize=10;fontColor=#0D47A1;" value="<b>Azure Standard Load Balancer</b><br>(Internal)<br><br>Frontend: <b>10.0.1.10</b> (static)<br>Rule: TCP 5022 → backend pool" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="95" width="270" x="480" y="145" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="haproxy" parent="1" x="530" y="478" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="sqlmi" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#BBDEFB;strokeColor=#0D47A1;strokeWidth=2;arcSize=10;" value="<font color="#0D47A1">🗄️ <b>Azure SQL Managed Instance</b><br>(Free tier, Entra-only auth)<br><br>FQDN: <b>&lt;mi-name&gt;.database.windows.net</b><br>Port: <b>5022</b> (MI Link / AG replication)<br>IP: Dynamic (can change on failover)</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="90" width="280" x="475" y="510" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="conn_haproxy_mi" edge="1" parent="1" source="haproxy" style="edgeLabel;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;resizable=0;points=[];labelBackgroundColor=#E6F0FF;" value="<font color="#2E7D32">TCP 5022<br><i>(FQDN-resolved, re-resolves every 10s)</i></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint as="offset" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="backend_subnet" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F3E5F5;strokeColor=#7B1FA2;strokeWidth=2;arcSize=8;dashed=1;" value="" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="220" width="310" x="830" y="270" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="backend_subnet_label" parent="1" style="text;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=top;resizable=0;points=[];autosize=1;" value="<font color="#4A148C"><b>backend-subnet</b> 10.0.2.0/24<br><i>(Simulated fallback — fast validation)</i></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="240" x="870" y="278" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="vm_sql_a" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#F3E5F5;strokeColor=#7B1FA2;strokeWidth=2;arcSize=10;" value="<font color="#4A148C">🖥️ <b>vm-sql-a</b><br>10.0.2.4 (static)<br>socat on :5022</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="65" width="130" x="850" y="330" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="vm_sql_b" parent="1" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="65" width="130" x="995" y="330" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="dns_zone" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#7B1FA2;strokeWidth=1;arcSize=10;dashed=1;" value="<font color="#4A148C">🌐 <b>Private DNS Zone</b><br>fake-sqlmi.database.windows.net<br>A: sqlmi-test → 10.0.2.4 (TTL 10s)</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="270" x="855" y="410" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="conn_haproxy_backend" edge="1" parent="1" source="haproxy" style="edgeStyle=orthogonalEdgeStyle;rounded=1;orthogonalLoop=1;jettySize=auto;html=1;strokeColor=#7B1FA2;strokeWidth=2;dashed=1;dashPattern=5 3;exitX=1;exitY=0.5;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;entryX=0;entryY=0.5;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;" target="vm_sql_a"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="conn_haproxy_backend_label" connectable="0" parent="conn_haproxy_backend" style="edgeLabel;html=1;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;resizable=0;points=[];labelBackgroundColor=#E6F0FF;" value="<font color="#7B1FA2"><i>Fallback path<br>TCP 5022</i></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry relative="1" as="geometry"> <mxPoint as="offset" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="key_note" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFDE7;strokeColor=#F9A825;strokeWidth=2;arcSize=8;align=left;spacingLeft=12;spacingRight=12;" value="<font style="font-size: 12px;"><b><font color="#0D47A1">Why This Works:</font></b><br><br><font color="#424242">• AWS firewall allow-lists a <b>single static IP</b>: <b>10.0.1.10</b> (LB frontend — never changes)<br>• HAProxy forwards TCP 5022 as a <b>Layer 4 proxy</b> — no HTTP required (App Gateway can&#39;t do this)<br>• HAProxy re-resolves the MI FQDN every <b>10 seconds</b> — backend IP changes are transparent<br>• Client never needs reconfiguration, even during MI maintenance or failover</font></font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="100" width="510" x="460" y="660" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="step1" parent="1" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#1565C0;strokeColor=none;fontColor=#FFFFFF;fontStyle=1;fontSize=12;" value="1" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="28" width="28" x="155" y="290" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="step2" parent="1" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#1565C0;strokeColor=none;fontColor=#FFFFFF;fontStyle=1;fontSize=12;" value="2" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="28" width="28" x="600" y="130" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="step3" parent="1" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#2E7D32;strokeColor=none;fontColor=#FFFFFF;fontStyle=1;fontSize=12;" value="3" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="28" width="28" x="600" y="260" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="step4" parent="1" style="ellipse;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#0D47A1;strokeColor=none;fontColor=#FFFFFF;fontStyle=1;fontSize=12;" value="4" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="28" width="28" x="600" y="440" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="step_legend" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#FFFFFF;strokeColor=#BDBDBD;strokeWidth=1;arcSize=8;align=left;spacingLeft=10;spacingRight=10;" value="<font style="font-size: 11px;" color="#424242"><b>Traffic Flow:</b><br>❶ Client sends TCP 5022 to static IP 10.0.1.10<br>❷ LB forwards to HAProxy backend pool<br>❸ HAProxy resolves MI FQDN, proxies TCP<br>❹ Traffic arrives at SQL MI on port 5022</font>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="80" width="310" x="30" y="470" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

div.div:
Azure Standard Load Balancer
(Internal)
Frontend: 10.0.1.10 (static)
Rule: TCP 5022 → backend pool

🔄 vm-haproxy
mode tcp (Layer 4)
Resolves MI FQDN via 168.63.129.16
hold valid 10s (re-resolves every 10s)

🗄️ Azure SQL Managed Instance
(Free tier, Entra-only auth)
FQDN: <mi-name>.database.windows.net
Port: 5022 (MI Link / AG replication)
IP: Dynamic (can change on failover)

🌐 Private DNS Zone
fake-sqlmi.database.windows.net
A: sqlmi-test → 10.0.2.4 (TTL 10s)

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe center; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding-top: 318px; margin-left: 772px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: center; color: #000000; background-color: #E6F0FF;
display: inline-block; font-size: 11px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; background-color: #E6F0FF; white-space: nowrap;
display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 484px; height: 1px; padding-top: 681px; margin-left: 445px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 12px; font-family: Helvetica; color: #000000; line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

font.font:
• AWS firewall allow-lists a single static IP: 10.0.1.10 (LB frontend — never changes)
• HAProxy forwards TCP 5022 as a Layer 4 proxy — no HTTP required (App Gateway can't do this)
• HAProxy re-resolves the MI FQDN every 10 seconds — backend IP changes are transparent
• Client never needs reconfiguration, even during MI maintenance or failover

Traffic Flow:
❶ Client sends TCP 5022 to static IP 10.0.1.10
❷ LB forwards to HAProxy backend pool
❸ HAProxy resolves MI FQDN, proxies TCP
❹ Traffic arrives at SQL MI on port 5022
